sections.paragraph:
Ἰωσήφ, ὁ indecl. (יוֹסֵף; Apollonius Molon [I b.c.] : 728 Fgm. 1, 3 Jac. [in Eus., PE 9, 19, 3]; Sb II 250, word-list; LXX; Test12Patr; JosAs; ApcEsdr p. 7, 10 and p. 32, 24 Tdf.; Philo; Just.; Mel., P. 59, 432.—EpArist: Ἰώσηφος; Jos. Ant. 2, 17; also C. Ap. 1, 92 Ἰώσηπος, ου) Joseph.

① the patriarch (Gen 30:24 and oft.; Test12Patr; JosAs; Demetr.: 722 Fgm. 1, 5 al. Jac; Philo, Mut. Nom. 90f and oft.; Just., D. 100, 1 al.; Mel., P. 59, 432) J 4:5; Ac 7:9, 13f, 18; Hb 11:21f; 1 Cl 4:9; B 13:4f; φυλὴ Ἰ. in Rv 7:8 stands for the half-tribe Ephraim which, w. its other half Manasseh vs. 6 brings the number of the tribes to twelve once more, after the loss of the tribe of Dan, to which acc. to tradition the Antichrist belongs (WBousset, Der Antichrist 1895, 112f).

② son of Jonam, in the genealogy of Jesus Lk 3:30.

③ son of Mattathias Lk 3:24.

④ husband of Mary the mother of Jesus (Just., D. 102, 2f; 103, 3) Mt 1:16 (BHHW II 886–89 [lit.]; PSchmiedel, PM 6, 1902, 88–93, SchTZ 31, 1914, 69–82; ibid. 32, 1915, 16–30; ERiggenbach, ibid. 31, 1914, 241–49; GKuhn, NKZ 34, 1923, 362–85; UHolzmeister, De S. Jos. Quaestiones Biblicae ’45), 18–20, 24; 2:13, 19; Lk 1:27; 2:4, 16, 33 v.l.; 3:23 (a genealogy in which the first name is given without the article, and all subsequent names have the article, as Theopomp. [IV b.c.]: 115 Fgm. 393 Jac.: in ascending order to Heracles; Diod S 5, 81, 6 Λέσβος ὁ Λαπίθου τοῦ Αἰόλου τοῦ Ἱππότου; Nicol. Dam.: 90 Fgm. 30 p. 343, 30 Jac. Δηιφόντῃ τῷ Ἀντιμάχου τοῦ Θρασυάνορος τοῦ Κτησίππου τοῦ Ἡρακλέους. Other exx. in Klostermann ad loc.; B-D-F §162, 2); 4:22; J 1:45; 6:42 (PMenoud, RTP 63, 1930, 275–84). GJs 9:1ff; 13:1ff; 14:1f; 15:1f, 4; 16:1ff; 17:1ff; 18:1; 19:1 (not pap); 21:1.

⑤ a brother of Jesus Mt 13:55. Cp. Ἰωσῆς 1.

⑥ Joseph of Arimathaea, member of the Sanhedrin, in whose tomb Jesus was buried Mt 27:57, 59; Mk 15:43, 45; Lk 23:50; J 19:38; GPt 6:23. Acc. to GPt 2:3 he was a friend of Pilate.—EvDobschütz, ZKG 23, 1902, 1–17.

⑦ Joseph, surnamed Barnabas Ac 4:36. S. Ἰωσῆς 3.

⑧ Joseph, surnamed Barsabbas (s. Βαρσαβ[β]ᾶς 1), also called Justus (s. Ἰοῦστος 1) Ac 1:23.

⑨ son of a certain Mary Mt 27:56 (s. Ἰωσῆς 2).

⑩ son of Joda Lk 3:26 v.l. (s. Ἰωσήχ).—M-M.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

oft. oft. = often

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

acc. to acc. to = according to

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

PM PM = Protestantische Monatshefte—List 6

SchTZ SchTZ = Schweizerische Theologische Zeitschrift—List 6

ibid. ibid. = ibidem (in the same book or passage)

NKZ NKZ = Neue Kirchliche Zeitschrift—List 6

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Theopomp Theopomp , hist., IV b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

exx. exx. = examples

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

RTP RTP = Revue de théologie et de philosophie—List 6

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

GPt GPt = Gospel of Peter—List 1

ZKG ZKG = Zeitschrift für Kirchengeschichte—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 486
